IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place in and around Upper Bourne End Lane. To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of trees near our overhead cables. This
has identified trees that have grown close, which could damage the cables and affect your electricity supply.
Our engineers need to safely cut the trees away from the cables. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide
you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power
temporarily. This work will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been
impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Please note we are connecting the generator early in the morning and
disconnecting the generator later in the evening due to local business units being affected.

equipment in your area. This has identified 4 sections of cables and 3 poles that need replacing
and upgrading. We will also be trimming trees around 2 sections of cables. Doing this will reduce
the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate some customers may
have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off. To ensure the power is off for the
shortest possible time, our contractors may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut.

We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place in and around Rushmere Road.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 12:20 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the George Street area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected underground fault, which our engineers diverted power around, while the repairs continue on site.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Aaron at 09:49 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the George Street area for safety so they can repair an underground network fault. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 12:30 and 13:30, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Aaron at 09:48 - Our engineers have been able to divert electricity around the faulty part of the network, restoring power to some customers. Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 18:51 - We can now confirm that all power in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to 360 customers in the New Lodge Chase area to safely repair some overhead equipment which had come down.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut today, our staff will work to restore your power as quickly and as safely as possible. A lot of our customers have questions regarding power cuts, and though you can always reach our team, you can also find some helpful information and answers to some of our most frequently asked questions on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/FAQ - Bear in mind this will use your mobile data, so feel free to speak to us if you'd prefer. We'll be in touch again once we have more information from our staff.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi David here from the messaging team. I'm so sorry for how long youve been without power for. I just wanted to let you know. Our extra team of specialist engineers have now arrived on site to work on repairs to the now known issue of an overhead electrical cable laying on the ground in the New Lodge Chase area. Due to the extent of work still required to repair and re attach the cable we have had to change the estimated timeframe to between 17:30 and 8:30.

We'll continue to keep you updated with all of our progress as it comes in from site. Sent by David at 16:13.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Sent by David at 16:10 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Fulbourn Old Drift CB1 area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault at the local substation which our engineers have replaced

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the Fulbourn Old Drift CB1 area.

As any issues that currently exist on our network have affected our equipment at the substation, engineers will start their testing there. They will then check our network equipment throughout the area to try and identify any problems, before trying to restore power.

Our estimated time frame of 17:30-18:30 hasn't changed, but if this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. This can involve ground excavations, traffic management equipment or specialist teams. As soon as our staff can safely update us, we'll give you the latest news. Sent by Chev at 15:39.

Sent by Lee at 15:21 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lee at 18:12 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
